title: Irlbachia tatei

The plant is classified within the tribe Griselinieae, a subfamily of the Amaryllidaceae family. Further classification includes the order Asparagales and the class Liliopsida. The classification of Irlbachia tatei reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Irlbachia tatei, also known as "Tate's Irlbachia," is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Irlbachia tatei include irlbachin, irlbachin A, and other annonaceous acetogenins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
